claimText: The International Union for the Protection of New Varieties of Plants (UPOV) is an intergovernmental organization that grants Breeder's Rights as an intellectual property right.
evidenceExcerpt: An intergovernmental organization to provide and promote an effective system of Plant Variety Protection (PVP). Grants Breeder's Rights (an IP right) to incentivize the development of new, improved plant varieties.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nature and function of UPOV.
distractorHooks: Stating UPOV is a non-governmental organization
Confusing Breeder's Rights with geographical indications

claimText: The UPOV Convention of 1991 makes the farmer's privilege to save seeds optional for its member countries.
evidenceExcerpt: UPOV Convention 1991: It strengthens breeders' rights and makes the farmer's privilege (to save seeds) optional for member countries.
examUse: Statement-based question on the provisions of the UPOV Convention 1991.
distractorHooks: Stating that UPOV 1991 makes farmer's privilege mandatory
Stating that UPOV completely bans seed saving for farmers

claimText: India is not a member of the International Union for the Protection of New Varieties of Plants (UPOV).
evidenceExcerpt: India's Status: Not a member and uses its own sui generis law
examUse: Statement-based question on India's membership in international agricultural and intellectual property organizations.
distractorHooks: Stating India is a founding member of UPOV
Stating India recently ratified the UPOV Convention 1991

claimText: India uses a sui generis law, the Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers' Rights Authority Act, 2001, to protect plant varieties instead of the UPOV framework.
evidenceExcerpt: India's Status: Not a member and uses its own sui generis law, the Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers' Rights Authority Act, 2001.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing India's domestic intellectual property laws with international frameworks.
distractorHooks: Stating India's plant variety protection is entirely governed by the UPOV Convention
Stating India has no domestic law for plant variety protection
